    Home › Records › Longest aircraft

    The 50 longest aircraft ever built

    Ranked by fuselage length — airliners, freighters, military giants, one flying boat and two supersonic transports. Number one no longer exists.

    Length is the record that tells the most stories. The top of this list is a graveyard of ambition: the An-225 destroyed in war after a life of world records, the Spruce Goose that flew for under a minute, Concorde and the Tu-144 racing each other into retirement. And the contest is still live — when the 777-9 enters service it will become the longest airliner ever to carry passengers, at 76.72 metres.

    Lengths are manufacturer figures for the longest variant named in each row. Aircraft covered in depth on this site are linked.

    The board, one to fifty

    #AircraftLengthFirst flightStatus
    1 Antonov An-225 Mriya84.00 m · 275 ft 7 in 1988Destroyed 2022 — one built 2 Boeing 777-976.72 m · 251 ft 8 in 2020In certification 3 Boeing 747-876.25 m · 250 ft 2 in 2010In service 4 Airbus m · 231 ft 10 in 1969In service 11 Boeing 777-869.79 m · 229 ft 0 in —In development 12 Antonov An-124 Ruslan69.10 m · 226 ft 8 in 1982In service 13 Boeing 787-1068.28 m · 224 ft 0 in 2017In production 14 Airbus A340-50067.93 m · 222 ft 10 in 2002In service (few) 15 Airbus A350-90066.80 m · 219 ft 2 in 2013In production 16 Hughes H-4 Hercules 'Spruce Goose'66.65 m · 218 ft 8 in 193 ft 0 in 1997In production 29 Antonov An-22 Antei57.92 m · 190 ft 0 in 1965Retired — largest turboprop ever 30 Douglas DC-8-61 / -6357.12 m · 187 ft 5 in 1966Retired 31 Boeing 787-856.72 m · 186 ft Boeing 767-300 / -300ER54.94 m · 180 ft 3 in 1986In service 38 Boeing 757-30054.47 m · 178 ft 8 in 1998In service 39 Lockheed L-1011 TriStar54.17 m · 177 ft 9 in 1970Retired 40 Tupolev Tu-16054.10 m · 177 ft 6 in 1981In service — longest combat aircraft 41 Tupolev Tu-11454.10 m · 177 ft 6 in 1957Retired 42 Airbus A300-60054.08 m · 177 ft 5 in 1983In service (freighters) 43 Bristol Brabazon53.95 m · Super VC1052.32 m · 171 ft 8 in 1964Retired 47 Boeing KC-46 Pegasus50.50 m · 165 ft 8 in 2014In service (military) 48 Boeing 767-20048.51 m · 159 ft 2 in 1981In service (freight/military) 49 Boeing B-52 Stratofortress48.50 m · 159 ft 1 in 1952In service since 1955 50 Ilyushin Il-7646.59 m · 152 ft 10 in 1971In service

    Compiled by aircraft.fyi from manufacturer specifications, verified 2026-07-14.

    Sources: Manufacturer specifications (Boeing, Airbus, Antonov, Lockheed, Tupolev, Ilyushin) · Cross-checked against type Wikipedia articles (CC BY-SA)

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
